claim_boundary: this report freezes the first concrete post-parity Parameter Golf architecture queue on the bounded local-reference harness. The implemented rows are post-train value-sharing probes over the frozen baseline control; they do not claim full retraining, challenge-speed improvement, single-H100 closure, or record-track readiness.

baseline_control.mechanism: Frozen local-reference baseline control with the current dense decoder, current Muon-plus-Adam split, and current int8-plus-zlib export.
baseline_control.boundary_note: This row remains the bounded local-reference control only. It does not imply single-H100 or 8xH100 challenge closure.

runtime_facts.runtime_note: This row is the frozen dense-runtime control for the current bounded harness.

variants.mechanism: Reuse one canonical decoder block by value across every decoder-depth position while leaving the current dense runtime path unchanged.
variants.boundary_note: This is a post-train shared-depth proxy, not a retrained recurrent runtime. It measures whether decoder-depth value reuse helps the shipped frontier under the same oracle and accounting surface.

runtime_facts.runtime_note: All decoder-depth executions still run, but only one decoder block remains unique by value in this proxy row.

variants.mechanism: Tie mirrored encoder-decoder block pairs by value so each pair shares one averaged block while keeping the current dense runtime path and exported-folder code surface unchanged.
variants.boundary_note: This row measures stronger block-level parameter tying on the frozen baseline family. It does not claim that the same tying scheme would remain optimal after full retraining.

runtime_facts.runtime_note: The dense runtime path stays unchanged; the only runtime-facing difference here is reduced unique block-state entropy in the exported artifact.

variants.mechanism: Evaluate one fixed local-attention or restricted-attention window on the frozen baseline family under the same metric and accounting surface.
variants.boundary_note: This row stays open until the repo owns a public-safe restricted-attention eval path on real seq_len=1024 challenge-format windows. Local-reference seq_len=4 evidence is not strong enough to close the issue honestly.
benchmark_plan: reuse the frozen baseline trained model and unchanged non-record code bytes
run restricted-attention eval on actual seq_len=1024 Parameter Golf token windows rather than only the local-reference fixture
preserve val_bpb, compressed-model bytes, and analytic or measured attention-work facts against the dense baseline row
